Your xtcode project in projects/xtcode/ is a good start but needs 3 things to be production-grade: 1) System prompt (prompt.py): expand to 500+ lines with detailed tool usage patterns, error handling strategies, multi-file editing workflow, when to ask vs act, search strategies, git conventions, security boundaries. Study your own engine/cortex.py system prompt as reference. 2) Git awareness: Add git_status, git_diff, git_commit, git_log tools. Wire /diff /commit /log commands to real tools. 3) Production polish: streaming output, error recovery with retry, context window management with auto-compact, /undo command using git. Track progress in brain/coding_scratchpad.md. Verify with tests. Checkpoint after each improvement.
retry

Fix xtcode LLM client in projects/xtcode/llm.py. It is broken - the Copilot API returns 400 invalid_request_body. Compare it line by line with your working engine/llm.py and fix the request format. The auth works (token is valid) but the message serialization is wrong. Also call_llm should be properly async. Test by running the fixed version.

CRITICAL BUG in xtcode: The LLM returns tool calls as XML text like <tool_call>{"name":"bash","arguments":{...}}</tool_call> inside the content string, but your code looks for structured tool_calls in the API response which are always empty. The Copilot API does not return structured tool_use blocks - it returns everything as text. Fix projects/xtcode/llm.py to parse <tool_call> tags from the response content text and extract them into the tool_calls list. Look at how your own engine/tools.py parse_and_execute works - it parses >>> TOOL() syntax from text. Do the same pattern for <tool_call> JSON tags. After fixing, TEST it end-to-end by creating a test script that: 1) Calls call_llm asking it to run a bash command 2) Verifies tool_calls list is not empty 3) Executes the tool call using execute_tool 4) Then do a full test: use xtcode to search the web for real news today and create newspaper.html Track progress in brain/coding_scratchpad.md. Checkpoint when fixed.
